More Notes: Small grains generally increased from low to intermediate species diversity (D; Equation 1) and declined at higher levels of diversity.
For maize, yields increased up to the maximum species diversity present in the dataset.
Yields increased with decreasing growing season temperature anomalies in all indicator crops.
Decreasing cumulated precipitation anomalies reduced yield anomalies of maize.
Decreasing cumulated precipitation anomalies was beneficial for spring small grain cereals.
Increasing anomalies of consecutive dry spells reduced yields for maize and winter small grain cereals.
The maximum yield benefit attainable via increasing species diversity was reduced, but not cancelled, by detrimental climatic conditions.
Spring small grains under first T quartile and first P quartile; assuming year 15 is 2015.
Winter small grains under first T quartile and first P quartile; assuming year 15 is 2015.
Maize under first P quartile and third T quartile; assuming year 15 is 2015.
